cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out which of the options A, B, C, or D that Vanessa Brown would agree with based on the provided text. First, I'll go through each choice carefully. Choice A: "Sunglasses deliver mystery to the observers as well as the wearers." The text mentions that sunglasses make the wearer feel more mysterious because they hide the eyes. Also, since the observers can't see the eyes, they can't form judgments about intelligence, confidence, sincerity, etc. So both the observer is kept in the dark and the wearer feels mysterious. That seems to match with A. The text says "And it works both ways, because the wearer of the sunglasses feels more mysterious, too." So A seems correct. Choice B: "Sunglasses were mainly used in risky sports after their commercialization." Wait, the text says "The way sunglasses were most used prior to their commercialization helps explain their inside coolness, Brown said, because in their early days sunglasses were primarily used during risky water and snow sports..." So the risky sports use was prior to commercialization, not after. So B is incorrect because it says "after commercialization" whereas the text says "prior to commercialization." Choice C: "Sunglasses are relatively modern and they became commonplace in the 1920s." The text says "Sales started to pick up in the 1920s, but they didn't become commonplace until about two decades after that." Two decades after the 1920s would be the 1940s, not the 1920s. So C is incorrect because it states they became commonplace in the 1920s, which is not accurate. Choice D: "Sunglasses cover up unbalanced oddities on the face to give people a sense of mystery." The text says that sunglasses make the face look more balanced and thus more attractive. But the mystery part is a separate point. The text says "Because of mystery.
